questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "If I had more free time, ___"
questions.answer: I would learn to paint.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "She would be healthier ___"
questions.answer: if she exercised more.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "If I knew the answer, ___"
questions.answer: I would tell you.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "He could travel abroad ___"
questions.answer: if he spoke another language.

questions.prompt: Complete the sentence: "If I were you, ___"
questions.answer: I wouldn't go out tonight.

questions.prompt: If I _____ (be) taller, I would play basketball.
questions.answer: were

questions.prompt: She would buy a house if she _____ (have) enough money.
questions.answer: had

questions.prompt: If they _____ (live) closer, we would see them more often.
questions.answer: lived

questions.prompt: He could get a better job if he _____ (speak) English.
questions.answer: spoke

questions.prompt: If I _____ (be) you, I would apologise immediately.
questions.answer: were

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "If I would have more money, I would travel the world."
questions.answer: No 'would' in the if-clause. Use past simple: 'If I had more money'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "She would be happy if she lives near the beach."
questions.answer: Second conditional needs past simple in the if-clause: 'if she lived'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "If I were you, I will look for a new job."
questions.answer: Second conditional uses 'would', not 'will': 'I would look for a new job'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "They wouldn't be late if they leaved earlier."
questions.answer: 'Leaved' is wrong. The past simple of 'leave' is 'left': 'if they left earlier'

questions.prompt: Find the mistake: "If he studyed harder, he would pass his exams."
questions.answer: 'Studyed' is a spelling mistake. The correct past simple is 'studied'
If I had more money, I would travel.
She would be happier if she lived closer.
He could get a better job if he spoke English.
If I were you, I would apologise.
What would you do if you won the lottery?
